title: My, your, his/her — possessive adjectives
objectives: Make them agree with the thing possessed (not the owner)

vocab.en: his / her / its / their / your (formal) — sg. / pl.

grammar_notes: Possessives agree with the THING possessed, not the owner — same as Russian (мой стол / моя книга).
mi/tu/su have only sg./pl. forms (no gender). nuestro/vuestro have full gender + number agreement.
'su' is ambiguous (his/her/its/their/your-formal) — context fills the gap.
Don't confuse 'tu' (your) with 'tú' (you) — the accent matters.
example_phrases: Mi madre es profesora. (My mum is a teacher.)
Tus libros están aquí. (Your books are here.)
Nuestra casa es pequeña. (Our house is small.)
